source_paper_title: Analysis of the Primal-Dual Central Path for Nonlinear Semidefinite Optimization Without the Nondegeneracy Condition
context:
Section 4 (Concluding remarks and future work), page 19

There exist two directions for future works. The first one is concerned with limiting behavior of the tangential direction \(\dot{x}(\mu)\) in the x-space as \(\mu \to 0\). We have the following conjecture: \(\lim_{\mu\to 0} \dot{x}(\mu) = \xi^*\), where \(\xi^*\) was defined in Proposition 5.
title: Conjectured limit of the tangential direction \(\dot x(\mu)\) of the NSDP central path as \(\mu\to 0\)
background_latex:
Consider the nonlinear semidefinite program (NSDP)
\[
\min_{x\in\mathbb{R}^n} f(x)\quad\text{s.t.}\quad G(x)\in S_+^m,\quad h(x)=0,
\]
where \(f:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}\), \(G:\mathbb{R}^n\to S^m\), and \(h:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}^s\) are twice continuously differentiable and \(S^m\) is the space of real \(m\times m\) symmetric matrices with cone \(S_+^m\) (resp. \(S_{++}^m\)) of positive semidefinite (resp. definite) matrices.

For \(\mu>0\), define a (primal-dual) central path as a smooth mapping \(w(\mu)=(x(\mu),Y(\mu),z(\mu))\in \mathbb{R}^n\times S^m\times\mathbb{R}^s\) satisfying the barrier KKT (BKKT) system
- stationarity: \(\nabla f(x(\mu)) - JG(x(\mu))^*Y(\mu) + \nabla h(x(\mu))z(\mu)=0\),
- primal feasibility: \(h(x(\mu))=0\),
- perturbed complementarity: \(G(x(\mu))Y(\mu)=\mu I\), with \(G(x(\mu))\in S_{++}^m\), \(Y(\mu)\in S_{++}^m\).
Assume the paper's standing conditions (strict complementarity, enhanced SOSC, and MFCQ) so that such a smooth central path exists for \(\mu\in(0,\bar\mu)\) and converges to a KKT triplet \(w_a=(x^*,Y^a,z^a)\) as \(\mu\to 0\).

Let \(\dot x(\mu):=\frac{d}{d\mu}x(\mu)\) denote the tangential (derivative) direction of the primal central path. Let \(\xi^*\in\mathbb{R}^n\) be the unique vector identified in the paper (Proposition 5) as the \(\Delta x\)-component of the solution set of a linearized system at \(w_a\): there exists \(\Delta Y\in S^m\) such that
- \(U_{x^*}^\top(\nabla_{xx}^2 L(w_a)\,\xi^* - JG(x^*)^*\Delta Y)=0\),
- \(L_{G(x^*)}(\Delta Y)+L_{Y^a}(\Delta G(x^*;\xi^*))=2I\),
- \(\nabla h(x^*)^\top\xi^*=0\),
where \(\Delta G(x;d):=\sum_{i=1}^n d_i \frac{\partial G(x)}{\partial x_i}\), \(L_X(W):=XW+WX\), \(L(w):=f(x)-G(x)\bullet Y+h(x)^\top z\), and \(U_{x^*}\) spans \(\{d\in\mathbb{R}^n: \Delta G_{EE}(x^*;d)=0,\ \nabla h(x^*)^\top d=0\}\) (block notation as in the paper's eigen-decomposition of \(G(x^*)\)).
problem_definition_latex:
Prove (or disprove) that for the smooth central path \(x(\mu)\) of the NSDP satisfying the BKKT system for all sufficiently small \(\mu>0\), the tangential direction converges and satisfies
\[
\lim_{\mu\to 0^+} \dot x(\mu) = \xi^*,
\]
where \(\dot x(\mu)=\frac{d}{d\mu}x(\mu)\) and \(\xi^*\in\mathbb{R}^n\) is the unique vector characterized above via the linearized system at the limiting KKT triplet \(w_a=(x^*,Y^a,z^a)\).
latex:
Consider the nonlinear semidefinite program (NSDP)
\[

\]
where \(f:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}\), \(G:\mathbb{R}^n\to S^m\), and \(h:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}^s\) are twice continuously differentiable and \(S^m\) is the space of real \(m\times m\) symmetric matrices with cone \(S_+^m\) (resp. \(S_{++}^m\)) of positive semidefinite (resp. definite) matrices.

For \(\mu>0\), define a (primal-dual) central path as a smooth mapping \(w(\mu)=(x(\mu),Y(\mu),z(\mu))\in \mathbb{R}^n\times S^m\times\mathbb{R}^s\) satisfying the barrier KKT (BKKT) system
- stationarity: \(\nabla f(x(\mu)) - JG(x(\mu))^*Y(\mu) + \nabla h(x(\mu))z(\mu)=0\),
- primal feasibility: \(h(x(\mu))=0\),
- perturbed complementarity: \(G(x(\mu))Y(\mu)=\mu I\), with \(G(x(\mu))\in S_{++}^m\), \(Y(\mu)\in S_{++}^m\).
Assume the paper's standing conditions (strict complementarity, enhanced SOSC, and MFCQ) so that such a smooth central path exists for \(\mu\in(0,\bar\mu)\) and converges to a KKT triplet \(w_a=(x^*,Y^a,z^a)\) as \(\mu\to 0\).

Let \(\dot x(\mu):=\frac{d}{d\mu}x(\mu)\) denote the tangential (derivative) direction of the primal central path. Let \(\xi^*\in\mathbb{R}^n\) be the unique vector identified in the paper (Proposition 5) as the \(\Delta x\)-component of the solution set of a linearized system at \(w_a\): there exists \(\Delta Y\in S^m\) such that
- \(U_{x^*}^\top(\nabla_{xx}^2 L(w_a)\,\xi^* - JG(x^*)^*\Delta Y)=0\),
- \(L_{G(x^*)}(\Delta Y)+L_{Y^a}(\Delta G(x^*;\xi^*))=2I\),
- \(\nabla h(x^*)^\top\xi^*=0\),

Prove (or disprove) that for the smooth central path \(x(\mu)\) of the NSDP satisfying the BKKT system for all sufficiently small \(\mu>0\), the tangential direction converges and satisfies
\[
\lim_{\mu\to 0^+} \dot x(\mu) = \xi^*,
\]
where \(\dot x(\mu)=\frac{d}{d\mu}x(\mu)\) and \(\xi^*\in\mathbb{R}^n\) is the unique vector characterized above via the linearized system at the limiting KKT triplet \(w_a=(x^*,Y^a,z^a)\).
